Tests if dividing positive infinity by negative infinity results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion that tests the behavior of dividing `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` by `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY` in JavaScript. It checks if the result of this division is `NaN` (Not-a-Number) and confirms that the expression evaluates to `true`. This assertion is useful for validating the expected behavior of arithmetic operations involving infinite values in JavaScript. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on this mathematical operation and its result.
